name: Peachberry Cobbler
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, mix cornstarch with a little of the raspberry syrup.
2. Add the remaining syrup, raspberries, peaches, and salt. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until thick and clear.
3. Pour into a 1 1/2-quart casserole or a 10 x 6 x 1 1/2 inch baking dish.
4. Prepare Drop Biscuits: With a fork, combine biscuit mix, sugar, milk, and butter, stirring well.
5. Drop batter from a tablespoon onto the hot fruit to make about 5 or 6 biscuits.
6. Sprinkle biscuits with sugar. Bake in a hot oven (400°F) for 20 minutes or until biscuits are done.
7. Serve warm with cream. Makes 5 or 6 servings.
Tasting-Test Kitchen note: You can use two 12-ounce packages of frozen peaches or one 1-pound can (2 cups) of sliced peaches. For either, include the syrup and then increase the cornstarch to 2 tablespoons.
